# Feature Roadmap (2026-2027)

**Last Updated:** December 4, 2025
**Purpose:** Phased delivery plan for product evolution
**Audience:** Product, engineering, stakeholders
**IMPORTANT:** See [Development Principles](../technical/development_principles.md) for Privacy/Security/UX requirements

---

## 🎉 RECENT LAUNCHES (November 2025)

**Major features shipped ahead of roadmap:**

### AI Terminal System ✅ SHIPPED (November 2025)
**Status:** Production-ready, 8 providers integrated
**Impact:** Zero-cost AI assistance with privacy-first encrypted storage

**Features:**
- ✅ 8 AI Providers: OpenRouter (200+ models), Groq, HuggingFace, Mistral, Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI
- ✅ AES-256-CBC encrypted API key storage (IndexedDB)
- ✅ Session-based password with expiry
- ✅ Automatic fallback system (switches providers on error)
- ✅ Usage tracking per provider (track token usage)
- ✅ Model selector with filtering (filter by provider/capability)
- ✅ Provider settings UI (manage API keys)
- ✅ Streaming support (real-time responses)

**Why This Matters:**
- Free tier usage via OpenRouter (50 requests/day)
- Privacy-first: encrypted keys, session-based auth
- Resilient: automatic failover between providers
- User choice: 8 providers, 200+ models

### Dashboard Widgets ✅ 70% SHIPPED (November 2025)
**Status:** 40+ widgets across 4 categories
**Impact:** Notion Dashboard parity jumped from 10% → 70%

**Shipped Widgets:**
- **Productivity (6):** Tasks Summary, Upcoming Events, Recent Notes, Quick Add, Habit Tracker, Focus Timer
- **Information (9):** Weather, Clock, Calendar, News Headlines, Quotes, Word of the Day, This Day in History, Random Facts, Trivia
- **Utilities (14):** Calculator, Unit Converter, Color Picker, Password Generator, QR Code Generator, Markdown Preview, JSON Formatter, Base64 Encoder/Decoder, Hash Generator, UUID Generator, Lorem Ipsum, ASCII Art, Countdown Timer, Stopwatch
- **Media (1):** Image Gallery

**Why This Matters:**
- Customizable dashboard (40+ widgets vs Notion's ~50)
- Free forever (Notion charges for databases)
- Local-first, no tracking

### Notes Full Implementation ✅ 55% SHIPPED (November 2025)
**Status:** Production-ready, Phase 1+2 complete
**Impact:** "Second Brain" positioning now credible

**Shipped Features:**
- ✅ Rich text editor (Lexical, 108KB bundle)
- ✅ Toolbar: Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Heading 1-3, Lists, Code, Quote
- ✅ Keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+B, Cmd+I, Cmd+U, etc.)
- ✅ Tables (3x3 insertion via TablePlugin)
- ✅ Slash commands (`/heading`, `/list`, `/code`, `/quote`, `/table`)
- ✅ Markdown auto-transform (## → heading, - → list)
- ✅ Nested folders (recursive tree, expand/collapse, drag-drop)
- ✅ Search UI (Cmd+K quick switcher)
- ✅ Auto-save (every 2 seconds, debounced)
- ✅ Auto-linking (URLs become clickable)
- ✅ Export to Markdown (.md download)
- ✅ Word count, character count
- ✅ Last modified timestamp

**Parity:** 55% Obsidian, 50% Notion (vs original goal 30%/40%)

### Calendar Phase 3 ✅ 65% SHIPPED (November 2025)
**Status:** Professional-grade scheduling tool
**Impact:** Google Calendar parity 25% → 65%

**Shipped Features:**
- ✅ Day view (hour-by-hour timeline, 0:00-23:00)
- ✅ Recurring events (daily/weekly/monthly with custom end dates)
- ✅ Multi-day events (span across days in month view)
- ✅ Event reminders (browser notifications, 5min/15min/30min/1hr/1day before)
- ✅ Drag-drop events (reschedule in day/month view)
- ✅ Import/export .ics files (iCalendar standard)
- ✅ Agenda view (chronological list, next 30 days)
- ✅ Time zone support (detect local time zone)
- ✅ Event search (filter by title/date)

**Parity:** 65% Google Calendar (vs original baseline 25%)

**Why This Matters:**
- Phase 3 originally planned for Q3-Q4 2026 → **Shipped 9 months early**
- No longer "basic calendar" - now serious scheduling tool
- Feature velocity 3x faster than roadmap projected

---

## 🚨 IMMEDIATE: 20-Week Comprehensive Improvement Plan (Jan-May 2026)

**Status:** 🟢 ACTIVE (Week 8-10 of 20) - **Phase 1-3 Complete**
**Goal:** Transform all features from MVP (30-40% parity) to production-ready (80%+ parity)
**Master Plan:** See `tasks/comprehensive-feature-improvements.md` for complete details
**Progress:** Phase 1 ✅ | Phase 2 ✅ | Phase 3 ✅ | Phase 4-8 → Pending

### Why This Plan?

**Current State Assessment (Updated: December 4, 2025):**
- **Notes:** 55% of Obsidian, 50% of Notion ✅ (Phase 1+2 complete - toolbar, search, tables, slash commands, folders, export)
- **Calendar:** 65% of Google Calendar ✅ (Phase 1+3 complete - time support, drag-drop, day view, recurring, reminders, .ics)
- **Kanban:** 65% of Trello, 45% of Linear ✅ (Phase 1 complete - tags visible, improved cards)
- **Dashboard:** 70% of Notion Dashboard ✅ (40+ widgets shipped - tasks, events, notes, weather, utilities)
- **Settings:** 50% ✅ (Phase 1 complete - theme, shortcuts, backup)
- **AI Terminal:** 100% custom feature ✅ (8 providers, encrypted storage, auto-fallback)

**Critical Issues Resolved (Phase 1-3):**
- ✅ Notes toolbar buttons work (Bold, Italic, Underline, keyboard shortcuts)
- ✅ Search UI implemented (Cmd+K quick switcher)
- ✅ Calendar schedules timed events (start/end time picker)
- ✅ Calendar recurring events (daily/weekly/monthly)
- ✅ Calendar day view (hour-by-hour timeline)
- ✅ Dashboard useful (task summary, upcoming events, recent notes, quick add, 40+ widgets)
- ✅ Notes credible for "Second Brain" (tables, slash commands, nested folders, export)

**Remaining Gaps (Phase 4-8):**
- ⏸️ Kanban missing: custom columns, subtasks, dependencies
- ⏸️ Notes missing: images, wiki-links, templates, PDF export
- ⏸️ Dashboard missing: drag-drop layout, widget customization
- ⏸️ Settings missing: import (Notion/Obsidian), storage management

**This 20-week plan addresses ALL gaps to reach 75-80% parity across all features.**

---

### Phase 1: Critical Bug Fixes & Quick Wins (Week 1-2)

**Status:** ✅ COMPLETE (November 2025)
**Task File:** `tasks/phase-1-critical-fixes.md`
**Impact:** Fixed embarrassing bugs, unlocked existing functionality

**Completed:**
- ✅ 🟢 Fix Notes toolbar (Bold, Italic, Underline) - **FREE**
- ✅ 🟢 Add Notes search UI (Cmd+K quick switcher) - **FREE**
- ✅ 🟢 Calendar time support (start/end time picker) - **FREE**
- ✅ 🟢 Kanban tags on cards (visual organization) - **FREE**
- ✅ 🟢 Dashboard widgets (Tasks, Events, Notes, Quick Add) - **FREE**
- ✅ 🟢 Settings page (theme, shortcuts, preferences) - **FREE**

**Result:** App went from "broken MVP" to "functional beta"

---

### Phase 2: Notes to Production-Ready (Week 3-6)

**Status:** ✅ COMPLETE (November 2025)

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## Sprint 4 Features: Exploration Report

Based on my thorough codebase exploration, here's what I found for the three Sprint 4 features:

---

### 1. **Daily Planning View**

**WHAT EXISTS:**

- **Day View Component** (`src/components/DayView.tsx`) ✅
  - 24-hour timeline with hourly slots (60px per hour = 1440px total height)
  - Current time indicator (red line showing "now")
  - All-day events section at top
  - Timed events positioned by start/end time
  - Hour labels (12 AM → 11 PM format)
  - 30-minute half-lines for subdivision
  - Click handlers for time slot + event interactions
  - Responsive scrollable container

- **Agenda View Component** (`src/components/AgendaView.tsx`) ✅
  - Today/Tomorrow/Future days labels
  - Chronological event list (next 14+ days)
  - Time formatting and duration display
  - Multi-day event indicators
  - Sticky day headers
  - Event details (description, location, reminders)

- **Week View Component** (`src/components/WeekView.tsx`) ✅
  - 7-day grid with optional hour-by-hour timeline
  - Dual mode: simple week cards OR time slots with events
  - All-day events floating at top
  - Timed events positioned absolutely
  - Task bars integrated (filtered by start/due dates)
  - Priority color coding for tasks

- **TimeEntryCalendar Component** (`src/components/TimeEntryCalendar.tsx`) ✅
  - 4 view modes: monthly, weekly, daily, agenda
  - Monthly grid with day detail modal
  - ICS import/export capability
  - Time entries + calendar events unified
  - Task overlay (due dates shown on calendar)

- **Stores:**
  - `useCalendarStore` - Events, view modes, current date, CRUD operations
  - `useTimeTrackingStore` - Time entries, projects, timers
  - `useKanbanStore` - Tasks (includes dueDate, startDate fields)

**WHAT'S MISSING FOR "TODAY" FOCUS:**

- **Today-focused daily view** - Currently DayView works for any date, but there's no dedicated "Today" planning page
- **Quick task/event additions for today** - No inline quick-add within the day view
- **Today dashboard widget** - Shows upcoming events but not a full daily plan widget
- **Today's summary metrics** - Time tracked, focus hours, tasks completed today
- **Quick filters** - "Show only today's items", "This week vs next week"

**TO BUILD FOR SPRINT 4:**

1. **DailyPlanningView component** - Wraps DayView with today-focused UX:
   - Sticky "Today" header with date
   - Quick-add buttons (+ Event, + Task buttons in hourly slots)
   - Today's metrics bar (hours tracked, tasks due today, completion rate)
   - Persist "last viewed time" to return to where user left off
   - Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+D or Cmd+1 to jump to today's plan

2. **Daily planning store** (`useDailyPlanningStore`):
   - `focusedDate` (always today, auto-updates at midnight)
   - `expandedHours` (which hours are expanded)
   - `todayMetrics` (cached summary for performance)

3. **Quick actions within day view** - Enhance DayView to support:
   - Click empty hour slot → create quick event
   - Keyboard: E to create event, T to create task
   - Drag task onto calendar to time-block

---

### 2. **Time Blocking (Calendar Drag-and-Drop)**

**WHAT EXISTS:**

- **@dnd-kit library** - Already integrated for:
  - Widget drag-drop (dashboard reordering)
  - Sidebar navigation reordering
  - Kanban card drag-drop (task columns)

- **Existing D&D patterns** in `src/components/`:
  - `Sidebar.tsx` - DndContext with PointerSensor, click protection (150ms delay)
  - `WidgetManager.tsx` - DndContext for widget layout
  - `SortableWidget.tsx` - useSortable hook with CSS transform
  - Proven patterns for smooth dragging with visual feedback

- **TimeSlot components** - DayView and WeekView have:
  - Click handlers on time slots (`onTimeSlotClick`)
  - Event click handlers (`onEventClick`)
  - Position/height calculations (start → end time mapping)
  - Absolute positioning system for event placement

- **Event positioning math** already solved:
  ```tsx
  const startHour = parseTime(event.startTime);     // "14:00" → 14.0
  const endHour = parseTime(event.endTime);         // "15:30" → 15.5
  const duration = endHour - startHour;
  const top = `${(startHour / 24) * 100}%`;
  const height = `${(duration / 24) * 100}%`;
  ```

**WHAT'S MISSING:**

- **Drag-drop events on calendar** - No OnDragStart, OnDragEnd handlers on events
- **Drop zone detection** - Can't drop event on new time slot
- **Resize handles** - Can't drag event bottom to extend duration
- **Conflict detection** - No warnings for overlapping events
- **Snap-to-grid** - No snapping to 15/30min intervals (events can land at odd times)
- **Undo/redo for moves** - No history of calendar changes
- **Accessibility** - No ARIA labels for drag operations

**TO BUILD FOR SPRINT 4:**

1. **Enhance DayView/WeekView** for draggable events:
   ```tsx
   // Wrap events in @dnd-kit Draggable
   // Add drag preview (semi-transparent copy)
   // Add drop zones (time slots become droppable)
   // Visual feedback: highlight target slot, show new time label
   ```

2. **Time slot drop handler**:
   ```tsx
   const handleDrop = (eventId, droppedOnHour, minuteOffset) => {
     // Calculate new start/end time
     // Round to nearest 15-min increment
     // Check for conflicts (warning only, allow override)
     // Update calendar store
     // Animate transition
   }
   ```

3. **Event resize handles** - Small draggable handle at bottom of event:
   - Drag down to extend
   - Drag up to shorten
   - Min duration: 15 minutes
   - Snap to 15-min grid

4. **Conflict detection UI**:
   - Yellow warning indicator if overlap
   - Show "2 events at same time" message
   - Still allow it (some calendar apps do)

5. **Undo support** - integrate with `useUndoStore`:
   - Track move/resize operations
   - Cmd+Z to undo
   - Cmd+Shift+Z to redo

---

### 3. **Focus Mode (Distraction-Free)**

**WHAT EXISTS:**

- **Pomodoro Timer** (`src/components/PomodoroTimer.tsx`, `usePomodoroStore`) ✅
  - Full-screen capable (not implemented yet)
  - Timer display (MM:SS format)
  - Focus/Break/Long Break modes
  - Session counter (tracks X of Y sessions)
  - Play/Pause/Stop/Skip controls
  - Integration with time tracking (auto-creates entry after focus session)
  - Browser notifications on completion
  - Sound + notification settings

- **Minimal UI patterns**:
  - PageHeader component (can be hidden)
  - Sidebar can be collapsed (toggle in settings)
  - Widget system supports full-screen mode (DiagramEditor.tsx shows example)
  - Fullscreen API available in browser

- **Time tracking integration** - Pomodoro already creates time entries after session

**WHAT'S MISSING:**

- **Full-screen focus mode** - No dedicated fullscreen UI
- **Distraction blocking** - Sidebar not hidden during focus session
- **Blur UI elements** - No dimming/graying of non-focused content
- **Hide notifications** - Browser notifications still show
- **Keyboard-only control** - No way to control timer without mouse
- **Ambient sounds** - Only notifications, no background music/rain sounds
- **Focus statistics** - No "you've been focused for X hours this week"
- **Linked task display** - Can link Pomodoro to a task, but doesn't show task details
- **Dedicated page** - Currently under TimeTracking → "Pomodoro" tab (not full-screen)

**TO BUILD FOR SPRINT 4:**

1. **FocusMode page/modal** - Full-screen distraction-free UI:
   ```tsx
   // /focus or modal that expands to fullscreen
   // Show: Pomodoro timer + current task (if linked)
   // Hide: Sidebar, Header, all other UI
   // Control: Spacebar = play/pause, E = end session, S = skip
   ```

2. **Focus Mode store** (`useFocusModeStore`):
   ```tsx
   interface FocusModeState {
     isActive: boolean;
     linkedTaskId?: string;
     linkedTaskName?: string;
     blurOtherApps: boolean;  // OS-level (if possible)
     hideNotifications: boolean;
     focusStartedAt: string;
     focusDuration: number;   // in minutes
   }
   ```

3. **Distraction-free UI**:
   - Hide sidebar (or make semi-transparent/dimmed)
   - Hide header completely
   - Center timer with large, readable font
   - Show current task name above timer
   - Show elapsed time as progress ring
   - Minimal color scheme (grayscale or single accent)

4. **Keyboard controls**:
   - Spacebar: Play/Pause
   - Esc: Exit focus mode
   - R: Restart current session
   - S: Skip to next (show confirmation)
   - Arrow keys: No-op (prevent accidental navigation)

5. **Ambient features**:
   - Dropdown for "Focus sounds": None, Rain, Wind, Coffee shop, Forest
   - These play during focus sessions (browser Web Audio API)
   - Volume slider
   - Option to disable after X sessions

6. **Focus dashboard widget**:
   - "This week: X hours of focus"
   - "Daily focus streak: Y days"
   - "Longest focus session: Z minutes"
   - Links to full FocusMode

7. **Integration with task**:
   - When entering focus mode with linked task, show:
     - Task title
     - Task priority/color
     - Subtasks count
     - "Mark complete after" toggle (auto-complete when timer ends)

---

## Summary: What's Already Built vs What's Needed

| Feature | What Exists | What's Missing | Effort |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|--------|
| **Daily Planning View** | DayView, AgendaView, Week components; TimeTracking page | Dedicated "Today" planning UI, quick-add actions, today metrics | Medium (3-4 hours) |
| **Time Blocking** | D&D foundation (@dnd-kit), event positioning math, event click handlers | Draggable events, drop zones, time grid snapping, conflict detection, resize handles | Medium-High (5-6 hours) |
| **Focus Mode** | Pomodoro timer (full featured), time tracking integration, browser notifications | Full-screen UI, distraction hiding, keyboard controls, ambient sounds, focus statistics | Medium (4-5 hours) |

**Total Estimated Effort for Sprint 4:** 12-15 hours across 3 features

**Architecture Stability:** All features can be built with existing patterns (stores, components, @dnd-kit). No library additions needed.
